input.headline: Is it true that … you burn more fat by working out on an empty stomach?

raw.headline: Is it true that … you burn more fat by working out on an empty stomach?

winner: I heard you lose weight faster if you exercise on an empty stomach, so I tried a new diet: no food, just running. Now my stomach is empty and so is my bank account—turns out, you burn more than fat when you're broke.

candidates.text: I tried working out on an empty stomach, and now I'm burning fat... and also my house. Turns out, I forgot to eat the fire extinguisher.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with a specific, relatable scenario; clever wordplay on 'burning fat' and 'burning the house'; concise and natural."}

candidates.text: Why did the empty stomach give up on fitness? It had no *guts* for cardio!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'guts' and 'cardio' are clever and specific, aligning well with the headline's topic. Brevity and surprise are effectively balanced."}

candidates.text: I tried working out on an empty stomach to burn more fat, and let me tell you, my abs got so toned they started doing their own taxes.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific with a clever twist on 'abs doing their own taxes.' It uses natural English and effectively combines the topic with humor."}

candidates.text: I heard you lose weight faster if you exercise on an empty stomach, so I tried a new diet: no food, just running. Now my stomach is empty and so is my bank account—turns out, you burn more than fat when you're broke.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Funny twist on the premise with a clever punchline that connects the joke to real-life consequences."}

candidates.text: I tried working out on an empty stomach to burn more fat, but I ended up just burning my dignity because I kept trying to bench press my own hunger.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific. It cleverly plays on the literal interpretation of 'burning fat' by twisting it into a humorous take on burning dignity, which adds a layer of unexpected humor."}

candidates.text: I tried working out on an empty stomach to burn more fat, but I think I’m just making myself a walking snack.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Funny and specific, with a clever twist on the idea of working out on an empty stomach."}

candidates.text: I tried working out on an empty stomach and burned so much fat, I had to eat a whole cake just to feel sorry for myself.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific. It uses a relatable scenario with a humorous twist that highlights the irony of burning fat only to eat more, making it both funny and natural."}

candidates.text: I tried working out on an empty stomach to burn more fat, but it turns out the only thing I actually burned was my motivation to keep going.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific. It cleverly subverts the expectation of burning fat by highlighting the loss of motivation instead, which is both humorous and relatable."}

candidates.text: I tried working out on an empty stomach — now I'm burning fat and my willpower.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "Puns on 'burning fat' and 'willpower' with a twist, concise and surprising."}
